description_long: This object is an elongated, irregularly shaped stone with a rough, natural surface. Its coloration features alternating bands of reddish-orange and pale gray, creating a striking striped pattern that runs along its length. The stone appears solid and dense, with jagged edges and a somewhat tapered form at one end. The surface texture is visibly coarse, with granular details and uneven contours that suggest it has not been artificially shaped or polished. The coloration and banding are consistent on all sides, giving it a distinctive, eye-catching appearance. This type of stone could serve as a natural barrier or decorative element in an urban landscape.
description: An elongated, rough stone with alternating reddish-orange and gray bands, featuring a coarse, natural surface and irregular shape.
description_view_0: The stone's broad side is visible, showcasing the prominent reddish-orange and gray banding and its uneven, jagged surface.
description_view_1: A narrow end view reveals the stone's thickness and the vertical alignment of the color bands.
description_view_2: The opposite broad side displays the full length of the stone and the continuity of the striped pattern.
description_view_3: A side profile highlights the stone's thinness and the layered appearance of the colored bands.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or as part of a low barrier
attribute_pattern: Distinct, irregular bands of reddish-orange and gray running lengthwise, resembling natural mineral layering.
attribute_weathering: Rough and unpolished, with granular texture indicating exposure to the elements but minimal biological growth.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved surfaces but can blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements in urban settings.
